accept_http1.description:
Allow this server to accept http1 requests.

Accepting http1 requests is only useful when developing `grpc-web`
enabled services. If this setting is set to `true` but services are
not correctly configured to handle grpc-web requests, your server may
return confusing (but correct) protocol errors.

Default is `false`.

concurrency_limit_per_connection.description: Set the concurrency limit applied to on requests inbound per connection.

http2_adaptive_window.description:
Sets whether to use an adaptive flow control. Defaults to false.
Enabling this will override the limits set in http2_initial_stream_window_size and
http2_initial_connection_window_size.

http2_keepalive_interval.description:
Set whether HTTP2 Ping frames are enabled on accepted connections.

If `None` is specified, HTTP2 keepalive is disabled, otherwise the duration
specified will be the time interval between HTTP2 Ping frames.
The timeout for receiving an acknowledgement of the keepalive ping
can be set with [`Server::http2_keepalive_timeout`].

Default is no HTTP2 keepalive (`None`)

http2_keepalive_timeout.description:
Sets a timeout for receiving an acknowledgement of the keepalive ping.

If the ping is not acknowledged within the timeout, the connection will be closed.
Does nothing if http2_keep_alive_interval is disabled.

Default is 20 seconds.

http2_max_header_list_size.description:
Sets the max size of received header frames.

This will default to whatever the default in hyper is. As of v1.4.1, it is 16 KiB.

http2_max_pending_accept_reset_streams.description:
Configures the maximum number of pending reset streams allowed before a GOAWAY will be sent.

This will default to whatever the default in h2 is. As of v0.3.17, it is 20.

See <https://github.com/hyperium/hyper/issues/2877> for more information.

initial_connection_window_size.description: Sets the max connection-level flow control for HTTP2

initial_stream_window_size.description:
Sets the [`SETTINGS_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE`][spec] option for HTTP2
stream-level flow control.

Default is 65,535

[spec]: https://httpwg.org/specs/rfc9113.html#InitialWindowSize

max_concurrent_streams.description:
Sets the [`SETTINGS_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS`][spec] option for HTTP2
connections.

max_connection_age.description:
Sets the maximum time option in milliseconds that a connection may exist

Default is no limit (`None`).

max_frame_size.description:
Sets the maximum frame size to use for HTTP2.

Passing `None` will do nothing.

If not set, will default from underlying transport.

tcp_keepalive.description:
Set whether TCP keepalive messages are enabled on accepted connections.

If `None` is specified, keepalive is disabled, otherwise the duration
specified will be the time to remain idle before sending TCP keepalive
probes.

Default is no keepalive (`None`)

tcp_nodelay.description: Set the value of `TCP_NODELAY` option for accepted connections. Enabled by default.

timeout.description: Set a timeout on for all request handlers.

oneOf.description: Retrieve and print the current wall-clock time.
Retrieve and print the relative elapsed wall-clock time since an epoch.
Formats local times and UTC times with FormatTime implementations that use the chrono crate.
Formats the current UTC time using a formatter from the chrono crate.

file.description: Set this if you want to write log to file

override_filter.description:
Override our custom tracing filter.

Set this to your own filter if you want to see traces from internal
libraries. See more [here](https://docs.rs/tracing-subscriber/latest/tracing_subscriber/filter/struct.EnvFilter.html#directives)

pretty_backtrace.description:
Enable nice display of backtraces, in development this should be on.
Turn it off in performance sensitive production deployments.

host.description: SMTP host. for example: localhost, smtp.gmail.com etc.

stub.description:
Use stub transport. This transport logs messages and always returns the given response.
It can be useful for testing purposes.

layers.description:
OpenDAL provides a variety of [layers](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/layers/index.html)

**Optional**: Need enable feature

scheme.description:
[Services](opendal::Scheme)  that OpenDAL supports

According trait [opendal::Scheme::from_str] to set the config,
visit [opendal](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/services/index.html) to learn more

**Required**: Need enable feature like `services-{$scheme}`

connect.description:
The URI for connecting to the postgres. For example:
* postgresql: `postgres://root:12341234@localhost:5432/myapp_development`

Please refer to [tokio_postgres::Config] for details.

connection_timeout.description: Each connection attempt to the server will time out after `connection_timeout`.

exponent_base.description:
The resulting duration is calculated by taking the base to the `n`-th power,
where `n` denotes the number of past attempts.

max_delay.description: Apply a maximum delay between connection attempts. The delay between attempts won't be longer than max_delay milliseconds.

number_of_retries.description: number_of_retries times, with an exponentially increasing delay

response_timeout.description: The new connection will time out operations after `response_timeout` has passed.

uri.description:
The URI for connecting to the Redis server. For example:
<redis://127.0.0.1/>

TokenStyle.description:
Token style for summer-sa-token

This is a local wrapper around the upstream TokenStyle to support JsonSchema

sa-token.description:
Sa-Token configuration for summer-rs

Most fields have sensible defaults.

# Example

```toml
[sa-token]
token_name = "Authorization"
timeout = 86400
auto_renew = true
```

active_timeout.description:
Token active timeout in seconds, -1 means no limit
Default: -1

enable_nonce.description:
Enable nonce for replay attack prevention
Default: false

is_concurrent.description:
Allow concurrent login for same account
Default: true

is_read_body.description:
Read token from body
Default: false

is_read_cookie.description:
Read token from cookie
Default: true

is_read_header.description:
Read token from header
Default: true

is_share.description:
Share token when multiple logins
Default: true

nonce_timeout.description:
Nonce timeout in seconds, -1 means use token timeout
Default: -1

refresh_token_timeout.description:
Refresh token timeout in seconds
Default: 604800 (7 days)

timeout.description:
Token timeout in seconds, -1 means permanent
Default: 2592000 (30 days)

token_name.description:
Token name (key in header or cookie)
Default: "Authorization"

sa-token.title: SaTokenConfig

acquire_timeout.description: Set the timeout for acquiring a connection

connect_timeout.description: Set the timeout duration when acquiring a connection

idle_timeout.description:
Set a maximum idle duration for individual connections.
Any connection that remains in the idle queue longer than this will be closed.
For usage-based database server billing, this can be a cost saver.

max_connections.description: Maximum number of connections for a pool

min_connections.description: Minimum number of connections for a pool

uri.description:
The URI for connecting to the database. For example:
* Postgres: `postgres://root:12341234@localhost:5432/myapp_development`
* Sqlite: `sqlite://db.sqlite?mode=rwc`

max_page_size.description:
Configures the maximum page size to be accepted.
This allows to put an upper boundary of the page size to prevent potential attacks trying to issue an OOM.
Defaults to 2000.

one_indexed.description:
Configures whether to expose and assume 1-based page number indexes in the request parameters.
Defaults to false, meaning a page number of 0 in the request equals the first page.
If this is set to true, a page number of 1 in the request will be considered the first page.

acquire_timeout.description: Set the timeout for acquiring a connection

connect_timeout.description: Set the timeout duration when acquiring a connection

idle_timeout.description:
Set a maximum idle duration for individual connections.
Any connection that remains in the idle queue longer than this will be closed.
For usage-based database server billing, this can be a cost saver.

max_connections.description: Maximum number of connections for a pool

min_connections.description: Minimum number of connections for a pool

uri.description:
The URI for connecting to the database. For example:
* Postgres: `postgres://root:12341234@localhost:5432/myapp_development`
* Sqlite: `sqlite://db.sqlite?mode=rwc`

oneOf.description:
`XREAD` with `NOACK`. This acknowledges messages as soon as they are fetched.
In the event of service restart, this will likely result in messages being skipped.

Auto ack and commit, but only after `auto_commit_delay` has passed since messages are read.

This is the default option, because users don't have to do anything extra. This also mimicks Kafka clients.

Do not auto ack, but continually commit acked messages to the server as new messages are read.
The consumer will not commit more often than `auto_commit_interval`.
You have to call [`RedisConsumer::ack`](crate::RedisConsumer::ack) manually.

This is the recommended option for achieving 'at-least-once' semantics.

Never auto ack or commit.
You have to call [`RedisConsumer::ack`](crate::RedisConsumer::ack) and [`RedisConsumer::commit`](crate::RedisConsumer::commit) manually.
Automatically reset the offset to the earliest offset.
Automatically reset the offset to the latest offset.
Throw exception to the consumer if no previous offset is found for the consumer's group.

AutoStreamReset.description: Where to start streaming from if there is no priori state.

oneOf.description: Use `0` as ID, which is the earliest message.
Use `$` as ID, which is the latest message.
This is the 'vanilla' stream consumer. It does not auto-commit, and thus only consumes messages from now on.
When the process restarts, it will resume the stream from the previous committed sequence.
You should assign a consumer group manually. The load-balancing mechanism is implementation-specific.
Fail if the file already exists
Consumers in the same group share the same shard

Consumers claim ownership of a shard

> This feature is still WIP

Contact.description: Contact information for the exposed API.

email.description:
The email address of the contact person/organization.
This MUST be in the format of an email address.

name.description: The identifying name of the contact person/organization.

url.description:
The URL pointing to the contact information.
This MUST be in the format of a URL.

EnableMiddleware.description:
A generic middleware configuration that can be enabled or
disabled.

Info.description:
The object provides metadata about the API.
The metadata MAY be used by the clients if needed,
and MAY be presented in editing or documentation generation tools for
convenience.

contact.description: The contact information for the exposed API.

description.description:
A description of the API.
CommonMark syntax MAY be used for rich text representation.

license.description: The license information for the exposed API.

summary.description: A short summary of the API.

termsOfService.description:
A URL to the Terms of Service for the API.
This MUST be in the format of a URL.

title.description: REQUIRED. The title of the application.

version.description:
REQUIRED. The version of the OpenAPI document (which is distinct from
the OpenAPI Specification version or the API implementation version).

License.description: License information for the exposed API.

identifier.description: An [SPDX](https://spdx.org/spdx-specification-21-web-version#h.jxpfx0ykyb60) license expression for the API. The `identifier` field is mutually exclusive of the `url` field.

name.description: REQUIRED. The license name used for the API.

url.description:
A URL to the license used for the API. This MUST be in the form of a
URL. The `url` field is mutually exclusive of the `identifier` field.

catch_panic.description: catch any code panic and log the error.

compression.description: Middleware that enable compression for the response.

limit_payload.description: Middleware that limit the payload request.

logger.description:
Middleware that improve the tracing logger and adding trace id for each
request.

timeout_request.description: Setting a global timeout for the requests

fallback.description:
Fallback page for a case when no asset exists (404). Useful for SPA
(single page app) where routes are virtual.

must_exist.description: Check that assets must exist on disk
